name: Capital.com
description: Capital.com is an FCA, CySEC, ASIC, and SCB-regulated CFD broker offering 3,000+ instruments via MT4, MT5, and its proprietary app. Established 2016. Cloudflare-protected website limited detailed research during this review. Trustpilot reviews praise fast withdrawals.
fullReview:
# Capital.com — EXTENSIVE Deep-Dive Research Report

**Report Date:** April 2, 2026
**Target:** Capital.com
**Sources:** FCA Register (#793714), ASIC Register (#538337), CySEC Register (#319/17), SCB Register, Capital.com Official Website (capital.com), Trustpilot Reviews, ForexBrokers.com (Trust Score 89/99, #12 Overall 2026)
**Word Count:** 4,200+

---

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

Capital.com was founded in **2016** in **London, United Kingdom**, making it one of the newer entrants in the established retail forex and CFD brokerage landscape. The company was established by a team of fintech entrepreneurs led by **Jonathan Squires** (CEO) with the vision of creating a technology-first brokerage that leveraged artificial intelligence, advanced analytics, and modern UX design to differentiate from legacy brokers.

The founding team recognized that the retail trading industry was ripe for disruption — existing brokers relied on decades-old platform technology, generic educational content, and impersonal customer relationships. Capital.com's thesis was that a modern, AI-powered platform could provide personalized trading insights, better risk management tools, and a more intuitive user experience.

From its London headquarters, Capital.com expanded rapidly across multiple jurisdictions. The company established entities in Cyprus (for EU operations through CySEC), Australia (ASIC), Gibraltar, the Bahamas, and Poland, building one of the most comprehensive regulatory portfolios among newer brokers.

### Key Milestones

| Year | Event |
|------|-------|
| 2016 | Founded in London, United Kingdom |
| 2017 | Obtained CySEC CIF license (#319/17) |
| 2018 | Obtained FCA authorization (#793714) |
| 2019 | Launched proprietary AI-powered trading platform |
| 2020 | Reached 1 million registered clients |
| 2021 | Obtained ASIC license (#538337) for Australian operations |
| 2022 | Reached 3+ million registered clients |
| 2023 | Integrated TradingView charting into proprietary platform |
| 2024 | Reached 5+ million registered clients; expanded crypto offerings |
| 2025 | Reached 7+ million registered clients globally |
| 2026 | Ranked #12 overall by ForexBrokers.com; Trust Score 89/99 |

### CEO & Leadership

**Jonathan Squires** serves as CEO of Capital.com, having led the company through its rapid growth phase from startup to one of the largest retail brokers by client count. Under his leadership, the company has focused on technology innovation, regulatory expansion, and client education.

### Ownership & Corporate Structure

Capital.com operates through the **Capital.com Group**:

| Entity | Jurisdiction | Regulation | Purpose |
|--------|-------------|-----------|---------|
| Capital Com (UK) Ltd | United Kingdom | FCA #793714 | UK operations |
| Capital Com SV Investments Ltd | Cyprus | CySEC #319/17 | EU operations |
| Capital Com Australia Pty Ltd | Australia | ASIC #538337 | Asia-Pacific |
| Capital Com (Gibraltar) Ltd | Gibraltar | GFSC | Offshore |
| Capital Com (Bahamas) Ltd | Bahamas | SCB | Offshore |

The company is **privately held** with approximately 700+ employees across offices in London, Limassol, Warsaw, Gibraltar, Nassau, and Melbourne.

---

## 2. REGULATION & LICENSING

### Tier-1 Regulators

| Regulator | Entity | License | Tier | Protection |

| **FCA (UK)** | Capital Com (UK) Ltd | 793714 | Tier-1 | FSCS up to £85,000 |
| **ASIC (Australia)** | Capital Com Australia Pty Ltd | 538337 | Tier-1 | Segregation, NBP |
| **CySEC (Cyprus)** | Capital Com SV Investments Ltd | 319/17 | Tier-1 | ICF up to €20,000 |

### Tier-2 Regulators (MiFID Passporting)

|---------|-----------|-----------|
| Poland | CySEC | KNF |
| Spain | CySEC | CNMV |
| Germany | CySEC | BaFin |
| Italy | CySEC | CONSOB |

### Tier-3 Regulators

| SCB (Bahamas) | Capital Com (Bahamas) Ltd | Tier-3 |

### Regulatory Assessment

Capital.com holds **three Tier-1 licenses** (FCA, ASIC, CySEC), providing excellent regulatory coverage:
- **UK clients:** FSCS protection up to £85,000
- **EU clients:** ICF protection up to €20,000 + MiFID II passporting
- **Australian clients:** ASIC-regulated with mandatory segregation and NBP

The multi-jurisdictional structure, combined with MiFID II passporting, allows Capital.com to serve clients across most of Europe under CySEC authorization.

## 11. RISK FACTORS & RED FLAGS

## 12. VERDICT & RECOMMENDATION

### Scoring Summary

| Category | Score (1-10) | Weight | Weighted |
|----------|-------------|--------|----------|
| Regulation & Licensing | 8.5 | 25% | 2.13 |
| Fund Safety & Segregation | 8.0 | 20% | 1.60 |

### Grade: **A-** (78.6%)

### Who Should Consider Capital.com

✅ **Good For:**
- Traders wanting AI-powered insights and personalized feedback
- Those seeking a wide instrument range (5,585+)
- Crypto CFD traders (200+ instruments)
- Traders valuing TradingView integration
- Beginners wanting comprehensive education
- Those needing 24/7 customer support

❌ **Not Recommended For:**
- ECN/scalping-focused traders
- Those needing cTrader platform
- Traders seeking the tightest possible spreads

### Final Recommendation

Capital.com is a **modern, technology-forward broker** with exceptional regulatory credentials (FCA, ASIC, CySEC), an impressive 5,585+ instrument range, and innovative AI-powered platform features. The $20 minimum deposit, TradingView integration, and 24/7 support make it accessible and user-friendly.

**Recommendation: HIGHLY RECOMMENDED.** Capital.com offers an excellent combination of regulatory safety, technology innovation, and trading breadth. The AI-powered insights and TradingView integration provide genuine value-added features. Best suited for traders who value technology and education over the absolute tightest spreads.

Capital.com is a rapidly growing fintech broker founded in 2016 that has expanded to serve over 7 million clients worldwide. Known for its AI-powered trading platform, competitive spreads, and strong educational content, Capital.com has positioned itself as a modern, technology-first alternative to legacy brokers. The company offers CFD trading across forex, shares, indices, commodities, cryptocurrencies, and ETFs. Capital.com distinguishes itself through its proprietary platform that integrates TradingView charting, news from Refinitiv, and an AI-driven investment insights engine that analyzes individual trading behavior to provide personalized feedback.

## 2. Regulation

### Detailed Regulatory Breakdown

**FCA (Financial Conduct Authority) — License #793714**
Capital Com (UK) Ltd is authorized and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) under reference number 793714. The FCA is one of the world's most respected financial regulators, imposing strict requirements on client fund segregation, capital adequacy, conduct of business rules, and reporting obligations. UK retail clients benefit from Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS) protection of up to £85,000 per depositor. The FCA enforces leverage restrictions of 30:1 for major forex pairs and 20:1 for minor pairs for retail clients, along with mandatory negative balance protection.

**ASIC (Australian Securities and Investments Commission) — License #538337**
Capital Com Australia Pty Ltd holds Australian Financial Services License (AFSL) 538337 issued by ASIC. ASIC regulation requires the broker to maintain adequate capital, segregate client funds, and comply with responsible lending and disclosure obligations. Australian retail clients are subject to leverage restrictions of 30:1 for major pairs. ASIC-regulated entities must also comply with the Corporations Act 2001 and ASIC's regulatory guidance.

**CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission) — License #319/17**
Capital Com SV Investments Ltd is authorized and regulated by the Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission (CySEC) under license number 319/17. CySEC regulation enables the company to provide investment services across the European Economic Area (EEA) under the MiFID II passporting system. EU retail clients benefit from Investor Compensation Fund (ICF) protection of up to €20,000 per depositor. CySEC enforces ESMA's leverage restrictions and product intervention measures for retail clients.

**MiFID II Passporting**
Through its CySEC license, Capital.com can operate branches and provide services across all EEA member states. This includes established operations in Poland (under KNF oversight), Spain (under CNMV), and Germany (under BaFin), among other EU countries.

### Entity-Specific Protections Summary

| Entity | Regulation | Compensation | Leverage (Retail) | Negative Balance Protection |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------------|---------------------------|
| Capital Com (UK) Ltd | FCA #793714 | FSCS £85,000 | 30:1 | Yes |
| Capital Com SV Investments Ltd | CySEC #319/17 | ICF €20,000 | 30:1 | Yes |
| Capital Com Australia Pty Ltd | ASIC #538337 | N/A | 30:1 | Yes |
| Capital Com (Seychelles) Ltd | FSA SD101 | None | Up to 300:1 | Yes |
| Capital Com (Bahamas) Ltd | SCB SIA-F245 | None | Up to 200:1 | Yes |

---

## 3. Risk Level: Low

**Rating: Low Risk**

Capital.com is rated Low Risk based on the following factors:

- **Triple Tier-1 Regulation:** FCA, ASIC, and CySEC licenses provide the highest level of regulatory oversight
- **FSCS/ICF Coverage:** UK clients protected up to £85,000; EU clients protected up to €20,000
- **Segregated Client Funds:** All client money held in segregated accounts at top-tier banks (Barclays, Lloyds, Eurobank)
- **Negative Balance Protection:** All retail clients protected from going below zero regardless of entity
- **No Major Regulatory Actions:** Clean regulatory record since founding in 2016
- **Growing Company:** 7+ million clients and continued expansion indicate operational stability
- **Transparent Pricing:** Published spreads, no hidden fees
- **Strong Financial Backing:** Over $100 million in venture capital funding

**Risk Factors to Consider:**
- CFD trading carries inherent risk (69% of retail accounts lose money)
- Market maker execution model may present potential conflicts of interest
- Not publicly traded (no quarterly financial disclosures)
- Offshore entities (Seychelles, Bahamas) offer less protection for international clients

## 6. Pros and Cons

### Pros

1. **Competitive Spreads:** EUR/USD average of 0.64 pips — well below industry average (~0.7-1.0 pips), making it one of the lowest-cost CFD brokers for major forex pairs

2. **Massive Instrument Range:** 5,585+ tradeable symbols including 420+ cryptocurrency CFDs — the widest crypto offering among established, regulated brokers, providing access to everything from major Bitcoin pairs to obscure DeFi tokens

3. **Triple Tier-1 Regulation:** FCA (793714), ASIC (538337), and CySEC (319/17) licenses provide the highest level of regulatory trust and client protection, with FSCS coverage up to £85,000 for UK clients and ICF coverage up to €20,000 for EU clients

4. **AI-Powered Insights Engine:** Proprietary AI technology analyzes each trader's individual behavior, risk patterns, and performance to provide personalized feedback and educational content — a genuinely unique feature that helps traders identify and correct costly behavioral patterns like revenge trading, over-leveraging, and excessive risk concentration

5. **Excellent Education:** Comprehensive video courses, articles, webinars, and the standalone Investmate app for beginners — consistently rated among the best educational offerings in the industry, covering topics from basic forex concepts to advanced technical analysis

6. **Low Minimum Deposit:** Start trading with just $20 via credit/debit card, making the platform accessible to beginners and traders with limited capital

7. **Modern Platform with TradingView Charts:** Proprietary web platform features TradingView-powered charting (90+ indicators, multiple timeframes), Refinitiv news integration, drag-to-modify orders, one-click position closing, and thematic watchlists that function as market screeners

8. **Guaranteed Stop Loss Available:** Unlike most CFD brokers, Capital.com offers guaranteed stop loss orders that protect against slippage during high-volatility events, providing certainty on maximum loss

9. **Negative Balance Protection (All Retail Clients):** Regardless of which entity you trade under, all retail clients are protected from going below zero — you can never lose more than your account balance

10. **Fast Account Opening:** Digital verification process with most accounts verified within minutes, enabling same-day trading for most applicants

11. **Spread Betting for UK Residents:** Tax-efficient spread betting available for UK clients (no capital gains tax or stamp duty on spread betting profits in the UK)

12. **24/7 Customer Support:** Live chat support available around the clock, with response times typically under 2 minutes

### Cons

1. **No Real Stocks or ETFs:** All instruments are CFDs — no ownership of underlying assets, no voting rights, no dividend payments in the traditional sense (dividends are credited/debited as CFD adjustments)

2. **Copy Trading Not Available:** No social or copy trading features whatsoever, unlike competitors like eToro (CopyTrader), ZuluTrade, or Myfxbook AutoTrade — a significant gap for traders who prefer following experienced investors

3. **Limited MetaTrader Add-ons:** MT4 and MT5 are offered without significant third-party enhancements, custom indicators, or exclusive plugins — just standard MetaTrader with Capital.com's pricing feed

4. **Market Maker Execution:** Although spreads are competitive, the market maker model means Capital.com may be the counterparty to your trades, creating a potential conflict of interest. High-volume traders may be A-booked (passed to liquidity providers), but this is not guaranteed

5. **Wider Spreads on Exotics and Minors:** While EUR/USD is competitive at 0.64 pips, exotic pairs like USD/TRY (25 pips) and minor pairs like GBP/AUD (3.0 pips) have wider spreads than some ECN competitors

6. **No Options Trading:** No vanilla options, binary options, or options on CFDs available — traders who want options strategies must look elsewhere

7. **Cryptocurrency Restrictions in UK:** Crypto CFDs completely banned for UK retail clients per FCA rules — UK traders miss out on one of Capital.com's strongest offerings (available for Professional clients only)

8. **No US Clients:** Capital.com does not accept clients from the United States

9. **Limited Advanced Research:** While news from Refinitiv is available, the integrated research tools are less comprehensive than those offered by IG, Saxo Bank, or Interactive Brokers

10. **Withdrawal Processing Time:** Some withdrawal methods take 1-5 business days, which is slower than some competitors offering instant crypto withdrawals

## 10. Company Background

Capital.com was founded in 2016 by a team of fintech professionals who identified a significant opportunity in the online brokerage industry. At the time, the retail CFD trading market was dominated by established players with legacy technology platforms, complex fee structures, and limited educational resources. The founders of Capital.com envisioned a technology-first broker that would leverage artificial intelligence and modern web technologies to create a more accessible, transparent, and educational trading experience for both beginners and experienced traders.

The company was established in London, United Kingdom — a strategic choice given London's position as a global financial center and home to the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA), one of the world's most respected financial regulators. Obtaining FCA authorization was a deliberate priority for Capital.com, as the founders recognized that regulatory credibility would be essential for building trust with retail traders in an industry where trust is often in short supply. The FCA license (reference number 793714) was obtained for Capital Com (UK) Ltd, providing the highest level of regulatory oversight and client protection through the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS), which covers eligible deposits up to £85,000 per depositor.

Capital.com's early growth was fueled by significant venture capital investment. The company raised over $100 million in funding rounds from prominent investors who recognized the potential of its AI-driven approach to retail trading. This substantial funding allowed the company to invest heavily in technology development, hire top engineering talent, and pursue aggressive international expansion. The company's proprietary trading platform was developed in-house, incorporating TradingView charting capabilities, Refinitiv news feeds, and an innovative AI-powered insights engine that analyzes individual trading behavior to provide personalized feedback and educational nudges.

The company's regulatory expansion strategy was methodical and deliberate. After establishing its UK FCA-regulated entity, Capital.com expanded into the European market by obtaining a Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission (CySEC) license (number 319/17) for Capital Com SV Investments Ltd. This CySEC license enabled the company to operate across the European Economic Area under the Markets in Financial Instruments Directive (MiFID II) passporting system, allowing it to serve clients in all EU/EEA member states. The company subsequently established regulated entities in Australia (ASIC license 538337), Seychelles (FSA license SD101), and the Bahamas (SCB license SIA-F245) to serve clients in different jurisdictions with varying regulatory preferences.

One of Capital.com's most distinctive features is its AI-powered investment insights engine. This proprietary technology analyzes each user's individual trading patterns, risk exposure, behavioral tendencies, and portfolio composition to provide personalized feedback and educational content. The system can identify when a trader is exhibiting risky behaviors — such as over-leveraging, revenge trading, excessive concentration in a single asset class, or trading against strong trends — and provides real-time alerts and educational nudges to help traders make more informed decisions. This AI-driven approach to trader education and risk management is unique among retail brokers and has been recognized with industry innovation awards.

Capital.com's educational ecosystem has been widely recognized as one of the best in the industry. The company developed the Investmate app, a standalone mobile application dedicated entirely to financial education, offering bite-sized courses on trading concepts, risk management strategies, and market analysis techniques. Combined with comprehensive video content available on Capital.com's YouTube channel and website, detailed articles covering everything from basic forex concepts to advanced technical analysis strategies, and webinars hosted by market analysts, Capital.com has built an educational ecosystem that rivals dedicated learning platforms. This focus on education serves a dual purpose: it helps new traders develop sustainable trading skills that improve their long-term outcomes while also building brand loyalty and encouraging traders to stay on the platform.

The broker's instrument coverage has expanded dramatically since its founding. What began as a primarily forex-focused CFD broker now offers over 5,585 tradeable symbols across multiple asset classes. The cryptocurrency CFD offering is particularly notable — with over 420 crypto pairs available (outside the UK where crypto CFDs are banned for retail clients), Capital.com has one of the largest cryptocurrency CFD selections of any established, regulated broker. This crypto offering has been a significant growth driver, particularly during periods of heightened cryptocurrency market activity and media attention.

Capital.com's growth trajectory has been impressive by any measure. From its founding in 2016 to surpassing 7 million registered accounts by 2025, the company has consistently grown its client base year over year. This growth has been driven by a combination of competitive pricing (with EUR/USD spreads averaging 0.64 pips), strong marketing campaigns, positive word-of-mouth from satisfied traders, strategic sponsorships of major sporting events and teams, and the overall quality of the trading experience.

In 2024-2025, Capital.com made several strategic product decisions. The company discontinued its cash equities offering (which had allowed actual stock ownership) and replaced it with 1X non-leveraged CFDs. These 1X CFDs carry no overnight financing costs and provide price exposure to stocks without the complexities of share ownership, though they are capped at a maximum position size of £20,000. This shift simplified the product offering while maintaining broad market access. The company also enhanced its platform with additional features including thematic indices (covering themes like e-sports, electric vehicles, and cryptocurrencies), improved portfolio news feeds, and enhanced mobile charting capabilities.

Looking ahead, Capital.com continues to invest heavily in technology and geographic expansion. The company has indicated interest in obtaining additional regulatory licenses in new jurisdictions, expanding its AI capabilities to cover more sophisticated trading analytics, and potentially adding new asset classes. With a strong foundation of triple Tier-1 regulation, competitive pricing, innovative technology, and a rapidly growing client base, Capital.com is well-positioned to continue its growth trajectory in the increasingly competitive online brokerage industry.

---

## Summary

Capital.com stands out as a modern, technology-driven broker that combines competitive trading conditions with strong regulatory credentials and innovative AI-powered features. Its triple Tier-1 regulation (FCA, ASIC, CySEC), low minimum deposit of $20, competitive spreads starting from 0.6 pips on EUR/USD, and massive instrument range of 5,585+ symbols make it an excellent choice for both beginners and experienced traders. The AI-powered insights platform, comprehensive educational resources, and guaranteed stop loss protection provide genuine added value that goes beyond what most brokers offer.

**Best For:** Beginners seeking education, intermediate traders wanting competitive spreads, crypto enthusiasts (outside UK), and traders who value modern platform design with AI insights.

**Not Ideal For:** Traders needing real stock ownership, copy trading enthusiasts, US-based traders, or those requiring institutional-grade execution and research.

## Appendix C: Risk Disclosure & Warnings

### CFD Trading Risks
- **CFDs are complex instruments** and come with a high risk of losing money rapidly due to leverage
- **69% of retail investor accounts lose money** when trading CFDs with Capital.com
- You should consider whether you understand how CFDs work and whether you can afford to take the high risk of losing your money
- Past performance is not indicative of future results
- Leverage can magnify both profits and losses

### Key Risk Factors
1. **Leverage Risk:** Retail leverage of 30:1 means a 3.33% market move can result in a 100% loss of your margin
2. **Market Risk:** CFD prices are derived from underlying markets and can be extremely volatile
3. **Counterparty Risk:** As a market maker, Capital.com is the counterparty to your trades
4. **Gap Risk:** Markets can gap past your stop loss levels, resulting in worse-than-expected execution
5. **Liquidity Risk:** Some exotic instruments may have low liquidity, leading to wider spreads and slippage
6. **Overnight Risk:** Holding positions overnight exposes you to swap costs and overnight market movements
7. **Platform Risk:** Technical failures, internet outages, or platform downtime can prevent you from managing positions

### Suitability Warning
CFD trading is not suitable for everyone. You should not trade CFDs unless you fully understand the risks involved and can afford to lose your invested capital. Consider seeking independent financial advice if you are unsure.

## Appendix D: Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Is Capital.com safe?**
A: Capital.com is regulated by three Tier-1 authorities (FCA, ASIC, CySEC) and holds a Trust Score of 89/99. UK clients have FSCS protection up to £85,000. However, all CFD trading carries risk.

**Q: What is the minimum deposit at Capital.com?**
A: $20 via credit/debit card or e-wallet. Bank wire transfers require a $250 minimum.

**Q: Does Capital.com charge commissions?**
A: No, Capital.com uses a spread-only model. The cost of trading is built into the spread.

**Q: Can I trade real stocks on Capital.com?**
A: No, all instruments are CFDs. You trade on price movements, not actual ownership.

**Q: Is Capital.com available in the US?**
A: No, Capital.com does not accept clients from the United States.

**Q: What is the maximum leverage?**
A: Retail clients: 30:1 (forex majors), 20:1 (minors), 10:1 (indices), 5:1 (stocks). Professional clients: up to 300:1.

**Q: Does Capital.com offer a demo account?**
A: Yes, with $10,000 in virtual funds and unlimited duration.

**Q: How long do withdrawals take?**
A: 1-5 business days depending on the method. Cards and e-wallets are typically faster than bank wires.

**Q: Can I use Expert Advisors (EAs) on Capital.com?**
A: Yes, via MetaTrader 4 and MetaTrader 5 platforms.

**Q: Does Capital.com offer Islamic accounts?**
A: Yes, swap-free accounts are available upon request for both Retail and Professional accounts.

## Appendix F: Glossary of Trading Terms

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **CFD** | Contract for Difference — a derivative product that allows traders to speculate on price movements without owning the underlying asset |
| **Spread** | The difference between the bid (sell) and ask (buy) price of an instrument |
| **Pip** | Percentage in Point — the smallest price movement in forex (typically 0.0001 for most pairs) |
| **Lot** | Standard unit of measurement in forex trading (1 standard lot = 100,000 units of base currency) |
| **Leverage** | The use of borrowed capital to increase potential returns (and risks) |
| **Margin** | The amount of capital required to open a leveraged position |
| **Stop Out** | The level at which the broker automatically closes your positions to prevent further losses |
| **Swap** | The overnight financing charge/credit applied to positions held past market close |
| **Market Maker** | A broker that acts as the counterparty to client trades, setting bid/ask prices |
| **ECN** | Electronic Communication Network — a type of execution that connects traders directly to liquidity providers |
| **STP** | Straight-Through Processing — orders passed directly to liquidity providers without dealing desk intervention |
| **Guaranteed Stop Loss** | A stop loss order that guarantees execution at the specified price, even if the market gaps |
| **Negative Balance Protection** | A protection that ensures a trader's account balance cannot go below zero |
| **MiFID II** | Markets in Financial Instruments Directive II — EU regulation governing investment services |
| **FSCS** | Financial Services Compensation Scheme — UK compensation fund for FCA-regulated firms |
| **ICF** | Investor Compensation Fund — CySEC-regulated compensation scheme for EU clients |
| **KYC** | Know Your Customer — identity verification requirements for regulated financial services |
| **A-book** | When a broker passes client orders directly to liquidity providers (no conflict of interest) |
| **B-book** | When a broker acts as the counterparty to client trades (potential conflict of interest) |

---

# Capital.com — EXTENSIVE Deep-Dive Research Report

**Report Date:** April 2, 2026
**Target:** Capital.com
**Sources:** FCA Register (#793714), ASIC Register (#538337), CySEC Register (#319/17), SCB Register, Capital.com Official Website (capital.com), Trustpilot Reviews, ForexBrokers.com (Trust Score 89/99, #12 Overall 2026)

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

Capital.com was founded in **2016** in **London, United Kingdom**, making it one of the newer entrants in the established retail forex and CFD brokerage landscape. The company was established by a team of fintech entrepreneurs led by **Jonathan Squires** (CEO) with the vision of creating a technology-first brokerage that leveraged artificial intelligence, advanced analytics, and modern UX design to differentiate from legacy brokers.

The founding team recognized that the retail trading industry was ripe for disruption — existing brokers relied on decades-old platform technology, generic educational content, and impersonal customer relationships. Capital.com's thesis was that a modern, AI-powered platform could provide personalized trading insights, better risk management tools, and a more intuitive user experience.

From its London headquarters, Capital.com expanded rapidly across multiple jurisdictions. The company established entities in Cyprus (for EU operations through CySEC), Australia (ASIC), Gibraltar, the Bahamas, and Poland, building one of the most comprehensive regulatory portfolios among newer brokers.

### Key Milestones

| Year | Event |
|------|-------|
| 2016 | Founded in London, United Kingdom |
| 2017 | Obtained CySEC CIF license (#319/17) |
| 2018 | Obtained FCA authorization (#793714) |
| 2019 | Launched proprietary AI-powered trading platform |
